message:
Add ruos-voice: Jarvis-style voice control daemon (Rust)

Pure Rust voice daemon with:
- Audio capture via cpal (ALSA/PipeWire)
- Energy-based VAD (voice activity detection)
- Whisper STT via whisper.cpp (142MB base model)
- 15+ voice commands: search, profile switch, app launch, status, security
- LLM fallback for unknown commands (via local Qwen2.5-3B)
- TTS responses via espeak-ng

Commands: "system status", "search for [query]", "switch to training mode",
"open browser", "lock screen", "run security test", "volume up/down",
"agent status", plus natural language via LLM.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

+[package]
+name = "ruos-voice"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2021"
+description = "ruOS Jarvis-style voice control daemon"
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "ruos-voice"
+path = "src/main.rs"
+
+[dependencies]
+# Audio capture
+cpal = "0.15"
+
+# Async
+tokio = { version = "1", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros", "process", "time", "sync"] }
+
+# HTTP client (for brain + LLM)
+reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["json", "native-tls"], default-features = false }
+
+# Serialization
+serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
+serde_json = "1"
+
+# CLI
+clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
+
+# Audio processing
+hound = "3"  # WAV read/write
+
+# Misc
+anyhow = "1"
+chrono = "0.4"

@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+//! Text-to-speech — speaks responses via espeak-ng.
+
+use std::process::Command;
+
+/// Speak text aloud using espeak-ng.
+pub fn speak(text: &str) {
+    if text.is_empty() { return; }
+
+    // Trim to reasonable length for speech
+    let spoken = if text.len() > 300 { &text[..300] } else { text };
+
+    // espeak-ng with natural-sounding settings
+    let _ = Command::new("espeak-ng")
+        .args([
+            "-v", "en",       // English voice
+            "-s", "160",      // Speed (words per minute)
+            "-p", "40",       // Pitch (lower = more natural)
+            "-a", "80",       // Amplitude
+            spoken,
+        ])
+        .output();
+}
